title: Epic schema and CRUD with project and tenant scoping

story.i_want_to: create, read, update, list, and delete epics within a project, where each epic has a number, title, description, phase, position, and metadata
story.so_that: I can organize a project's work into logical groups (epics) that map to feature areas or development phases

rationale: Epics are the intermediate grouping between projects and stories. They represent feature areas or phases of development (e.g., 'Foundation', 'Authentication', 'Work Breakdown'). The number field provides a stable human-readable identifier (Epic 1, Epic 2). The phase field enables grouping epics by development phase. Position enables custom ordering within a phase. Epics are scoped to both a project and a tenant.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.WorkBreakdown.Epic schema exists with fields: id, tenant_id, project_id, number (integer), title, description, phase, position (integer), metadata (jsonb), inserted_at, updated_at
The Epic schema uses Loopctl.Schema with tenant_id required; number is unique within a project (composite unique index on [project_id, number])
POST /api/v1/projects/:project_id/epics (role: user) creates an epic; requires number and title, optional description, phase, position, metadata
GET /api/v1/projects/:project_id/epics (role: agent+) lists all epics for a project, ordered by position then number, with story count and completion percentage per epic
GET /api/v1/epics/:id (role: agent+) returns a single epic with its stories preloaded
PATCH /api/v1/epics/:id (role: user) updates epic fields; number cannot be changed after creation
DELETE /api/v1/epics/:id (role: user) deletes the epic and cascades deletion to all child stories
GET /api/v1/epics/:id/progress (role: agent+) returns epic-level progress: story count by agent_status and verified_status
All queries are scoped to the current tenant via both application-level WHERE clause and RLS policy
RLS policy is enabled on the epics table enforcing tenant isolation at the database level
Every mutation (create, update, delete) MUST be recorded in the audit log via Ecto.Multi in the same transaction as the entity change.

test_cases.name: Create epic with valid attributes
expected_results: Response body has epic with id, number = 1, title = "Foundation", project_id
Epic exists in database with correct tenant_id and project_id

test_cases.name: Reject duplicate epic number within project
expected_results: Response status is 409 or 422 with error on number

test_cases.name: Allow same epic number in different projects
steps: Create epic with number 1 in project_b
expected_results: Epic creation succeeds — number uniqueness is per-project

test_cases.name: List epics returns story counts and completion
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, number: 1}) as epic
expected_results: Response contains epic with story_count = 2 and completion_percentage = 50.0

test_cases.name: Delete epic cascades to stories
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic

test_cases.name: Epic queries are tenant-scoped
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant_b.id, project_id: project_b.id}) as other_epic
expected_results: Response status is 404 (cannot access another tenant's epic)

test_cases.name: Epic progress endpoint returns status breakdown
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id, agent_status: :pending, verified_status: :unverified})
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id, agent_status: :reported_done, verified_status: :verified})
expected_results: Response includes stories_by_agent_status and stories_by_verified_status breakdowns

test_cases.name: Mutation creates audit log entry
steps: Perform the mutation via API endpoint
expected_results: Audit log entry created with correct entity_type, entity_id, action, and actor
Audit entry is in the same transaction (no orphaned entity without audit)

technical_notes: Phase values are free-form strings, not an enum — allows flexibility for different project structures
Position defaults to 0 if not specified; used for ordering within a phase
Cascade delete: use on_delete: :delete_all in the migration foreign key for stories → epics
Consider a Loopctl.WorkBreakdown context module that handles both epics and stories
The list endpoint should support optional filtering by phase: ?phase=p0_foundation
Epic number is an integer (not a string) unlike story numbers which are strings (e.g., '2.1')
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
